meta.note: Rebuilt from verified awards.json data. Card counts from roster.json (138-artist subset) or best known figure — flagged where approximate. Collection status current as of June 2026.

qualifying_credentials: Spectrum Fantastic Art — Gold Medal (any category)
Society of Illustrators — Gold Medal (NY or LA)
National Portrait Gallery London — selected exhibition (BP Portrait Award)
exceptions: OG MTG Lane — Alpha-era artist with personal/historical significance (case-by-case)
Exceptional gallery CV — MoCA / major museum exhibition level (case-by-case)
exclusions: Nominations without wins do NOT qualify (except NPG London and Comm Arts which are selection-based)
standalone_credentials: Norman Rockwell Museum 'Enchanted: A History of Fantasy Illustration' (2020-21) — EXHIBITED. Qualifies under the museum-exhibition exception; Gene weights it highly.

meta.reconciled: Reconciled against July 2026 credential audit + roster-field sweep. Kept live-file data (credentials, mtg_cards, ap_store); applied corrections + added fields.

field_conventions.nrm_enchanted: Split by permanence: 'plate' = reproduced in the Abbeville Enchanted catalog (durable published record; assigned from publisher's showcased-artist list — Gene to confirm exact plates from his copy). 'exhibited_non_plate' = in the show / listed in book but art NOT reproduced (e.g. Omar Rayyan). 'attended_unconfirmed' = at opening, exhibition inclusion unconfirmed (Guay). Score: plate 10, non_plate 5, attended 0.
field_conventions.instagram_followers: verified ~7/2026; null=not gathered/negligible. Demand-liquidity signal, NOT prestige.
field_conventions.gallery_museum: museum / strong_gallery / gallery / null. Separate from nrm_enchanted.
field_conventions.privacy: No acquisition prices / cost basis in this file — per storage rule, cost data lives in memory + locked Notes only, never the public repo.
field_conventions.obtainable: yes / verify / limited / drops-only / no. 'no' = tier-one by credential but no realistic painted-AP or commission pathway (e.g. Michael Whelan: MTG only via Secret Lair, does not commission). Record-only/aspirational.
field_conventions.institutional_tier: museum / gallery+ / gallery / circuit / none. 'gallery' now includes juried IX Main Show (IlluxCon) exhibitors and Character in Context (A.R. Mitchell Museum) — real recurring fantasy-illustration exhibition credentials, distinct from the non-juried IX Showcase (circuit).
field_conventions.documentary_footprint: A=monograph/museum-catalog/Wikipedia/institution-level; B=major-publication features + own art books + gallery press; C=minimal after fair look (the cull). Venue quality over count.
field_conventions.durability_ranking: art-historian weighting = institutional_tier + nrm_enchanted + documentary_footprint + exceptional-credential + career-legacy. Social followers and obtainability are DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (kept as separate columns) so peak-footprint/no-access artists (e.g. Whelan) stay visible.

open_items: RESIDUAL (needs Gene's physical Enchanted catalog): confirm exact plates vs the showcased-artist inference for the 12 'plate' artists.

HELD (not added to roster): Vance Kovacs (54 MTG cards but no confirmed gate award — add if a Chesley/Spectrum win surfaces). Kinuko Craft RESOLVED — excluded, no MTG cards. Charles Vess excluded — no confirmed MTG cards.
obtainability 'inquiry' (McCaig, Martiniere, Ortiz, Sheppard, Jaime Jones) = no public AP pathway; resolvable only by contacting the artist.
All other verification complete: award gate, MTG status, documentary footprint (fair look), NRM plate/non-plate split, data conflicts (Palumbo Chesley confirmed, Andrade AD claim removed).
meta.sweep_complete: 2026-07 — credential-first intersection run across all gate awards.

documentary_sources_taxonomy.institutional: Museum programming of record: NRM 'The Illustrator's Studio' podcast (Kowalski) + Enchanted catalog/exhibition; Society of Illustrators; ARC videos; monographs; museum-catalog essays; Wikipedia.
documentary_sources_taxonomy.independent_documentary_criticism: Rhystic Studies (Sam Gaglio — dedicated video essays + Substack profiles); Spice8Rack; The Mirror Gallery / Vorthos Mike (Mike Linnemann, Hipsters of the Coast) MTG art criticism + interviews.
documentary_sources_taxonomy.official_art_of_record: 'The Art of Magic: The Gathering' set-book series (VIZ/Wizards).
documentary_sources_taxonomy.interview_podcast: Humans of Magic (James Hsu); MTG Artist Connection; Vorthos Cast.
documentary_sources_taxonomy.fine_art_press: ImagineFX, Communication Arts, Spectrum written essays.
documentary_sources_taxonomy.note: CORRECTION 7/2026: original documentary sweep used fine-art sources only, structurally under-counting MTG-native artists. Recalibrated to weigh MTG-native documentary. 'C' for a working MTG artist now means 'no dedicated feature found yet', not 'no footprint'. Per-artist episode-level mapping across Rhystic Studies / Mirror Gallery / Art-of-MTG / podcasts still outstanding for the flagged names.
documentary_sources_taxonomy.documentary_films: Eye of the Beholder: The Art of Dungeons & Dragons (2019) — subjects incl. Brom, Erol Otus. Igniting the Spark: The Story of Magic: The Gathering (2025, Gen Con Best Doc) — subjects incl. Donato, Rob Alexander, Winona Nelson, Jarel Threat.
documentary_sources_taxonomy.dnd_canon: Art & Arcana: A Visual History (2018) = definitive D&D art-history book; Worlds & Realms (2024); Dragon/Dungeon archives. D&D-lane artists: Erol Otus, Todd Lockwood, Tyler Jacobson, Justin Sweet, Ryan Pancoast, Kekai Kotaki.

prestige_score.scale: 0-100 (capped), artist-level. Durability of art-historical standing for a long hold — NOT price/return.
prestige_score.model: pillars (Credentials 30 + Institutional 45 + Documentary 10 + Legacy 5 = max 90) + additive Enchanted + additive Koder/Eidolon + additive Lucas/comics, capped at 100.
prestige_score.enchanted_element: ADDITIVE (rewards inclusion, does not penalize absence). = plate/non-plate base (plate 20 / non-plate 10 / attended 0) + online-presence supplement (0-5 = count of documented exhibit signal types: press-image, review, podcast, symposium, blog, made-for-show). Reflects Gene's thesis that NRM 'Enchanted' is a seminal, canon-defining first survey of fantasy illustration.
prestige_score.excluded_by_design: social followers, obtainability, cost, card-count — separate decision axes.
prestige_score.caveats: Plate list is inferred from publisher showcased-artist list (confirm vs physical catalog). Presence signals are documented-footprint counts, not literal mention totals. Weights tunable.
prestige_score.usage: Triangulate prestige x obtainable x cost x card iconicity for buy decisions.
prestige_score.koder_eidolon_element: ADDITIVE, at NRM-Enchanted-plate parity (Gene's belief: inclusion in the Koder Collection / forthcoming Eidolon Museum canonizes like inclusion in the Norman Rockwell 'Enchanted' show). The Koder Collection ('world's most complete D&D/fantasy-game art collection', owner Matthew Koder) is curated/catalogued by Jon Peterson (co-author, Art & Arcana) + art historian Jessica Lee Patterson; destined for the Eidolon Museum (Altoona PA, dir. Patrick Wilshire/IX). status = confirmed 20 (verified in collection: Brom, Todd Lockwood via the 2024 Lucca 'Gateway to Adventure' show) / proxy_defining 20 (D&D-canon-defining, near-certain inclusion, used when explicit confirmation unavailable: Erol Otus, Tyler Jacobson) / proxy_major 10 / proxy_contributor 5. NOT a separate 'D&D reputation' axis — the proxy tiers ARE the estimate of likely Koder/Eidolon inclusion, upgraded to 'confirmed' when verified (via Lucca show, Patterson's catalog, or the Art & Arcana index). Vetting list: Art & Arcana artist index. Tunable.
prestige_score.weights_note: Documentary reduced to interview-scale (A=10 ≈ one NRM 'Illustrator's Studio' interview; B=5; C=2) and the freed weight reassigned to Institutional (museum 45 / gallery+ 30 / gallery 20 / circuit 9 / none 0). Rationale: institutional presence is the durable signal; documentary footprint is supporting.
prestige_score.koder_vetting_note: Art & Arcana artist index used as the Koder/Eidolon vetting proxy (Peterson curates both). Result: marquee D&D artists who are also MTG artists are already in the roster (Koder batch). Non-MTG OG D&D canon artists (Trampier, Sutherland, Dee, Darlene, Jaquays, Willingham) excluded — no MTG cards. Second-hand Lucca coverage (reviews/panel) names only the already-confirmed set + 'many others' unnamed; it amplified Otus & Tyler Jacobson to confirmed. HOLD for verification (D&D-canon + MTG, but Koder/Art&Arcana inclusion + gate unconfirmed): Fred Fields, Alan Pollack, Ralph Horsley, Steve Prescott, Michael Komarck.
prestige_score.lucas_narrative_element: ADDITIVE comics-canon credential anchored on the Lucas Museum of Narrative Art (LA; already acquired Julie Bell & Boris Vallejo). CONFIRMED Lucas Museum acquisition = MAXES the Institutional pillar (sets institutional_tier=museum=45) rather than stacking a separate additive — Lucas inclusion IS top-tier museum-collection inclusion, so it's counted once, in the institutional pillar. proxy_eisner = 6 additive (Eisner HoF or win = comics canon; Chesley-Lifetime level = 5x single-year win). Vetting gate = Eisner. Only applies to artists who are ALSO MTG artists. Tunable.

data_notes.roster_incompleteness: roster.json (casualplaneswalker) is INCOMPLETE: verified-MTG artists missing from it include Larry Elmore, Jeff Easley, Keith Parkinson, Tim Hildebrandt, Robh Ruppel, Michael Kaluta, Julie Bell (all confirmed via Scryfall). awards.json inherits this gap (built from the roster intersection) so it omits their real Chesley wins (Berry, DiTerlizzi, Bell — verified via ASFA/sfadb). Card counts mix sources: roster.json=all-printings where present, Scryfall unique:art where absent (noted per artist). ACTION: supplement roster.json with these artists.

Karla Ortiz.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). MTG (Ghoulcaller Gisa, planeswalkers). High-profile AI-rights advocate; AP pathway to verify.
Karla Ortiz.documentary_note: ImagineFX interview/tutorial; high-profile (film concept, AI-rights discourse).
Karla Ortiz.obtainable_note: No public AP/commission pathway found; requires direct inquiry.

Cynthia Sheppard.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). MTG artist + former WotC Art Director; AP pathway to verify.
Cynthia Sheppard.obtainable_note: No public AP/commission pathway found; requires direct inquiry.

Iain McCaig.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). MTG since 2002 (Throne of Eldraine return); Star Wars concept legend. Likely limited AP access.
Iain McCaig.documentary_note: Star Wars concept legend; art books; Spectrum Grandmaster; ImagineFX.
Iain McCaig.obtainable_note: No public AP/commission pathway found; requires direct inquiry.

Rob Alexander.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). Prolific early MTG lands artist. Does signings/APs.
Rob Alexander.documentary_note: 'Igniting the Spark' MTG documentary (interviewed subject); prolific early MTG lands artist. (Bumped from C-pending.)

Michael C. Hayes.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). Won Chesley FOR an MTG card. Traditional oils.
Michael C. Hayes.documentary_note: C — fair look completed across MTG-native sources (Secret Lair Artist Series, Mirror Gallery, Art of Magic books, Rhystic/podcasts); credentialed but not a documentary subject. C is accurate.

Wylie Beckert.documentary_note: C — fair look completed across MTG-native sources (Secret Lair Artist Series, Mirror Gallery, Art of Magic books, Rhystic/podcasts); credentialed but not a documentary subject. C is accurate.

Mark Zug.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). Iconic older MTG artist (many classics). Signings.
Mark Zug.documentary_note: NRM Enchanted exhibition + catalog (museum documentary); OG-era MTG documentary presence. (Bumped from C.)

Brom.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). Major name; gallery/collector market, expensive, painted APs rare.
Brom.documentary_note: Own art books + illustrated novels; 'Eye of the Beholder' documentary + 'Art & Arcana' (D&D canon); ImagineFX; named influence on Karla Ortiz.
Brom.enchanted_presence_signals: The Night Mare pictured in review gallery

Jaime Jones.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). Major modern MTG artist; largely digital; AP pathway to verify.

Stephan Martiniere.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). MTG artist; largely digital sci-fi; AP pathway to verify.

Stephan Martiniere.obtainable_note: No public AP/commission pathway found; requires direct inquiry.

Michael Whelan.credentials: Chesley x13 + Lifetime Achievement; Spectrum Gold x4 + Grandmaster 2004; multiple Hugo; World Fantasy.
Michael Whelan.notes: GAP (sweep 7/2026). Tier-one by credential (one of the most-decorated SF artists ever) BUT MTG only via Secret Lair; does NOT do commissions/painted-AP work — effectively UNOBTAINABLE. Aspirational/record-only.
Michael Whelan.documentary_note: 5 monographs incl. major retrospectives; Wikipedia; SF Hall of Fame (1st living inductee); Society of Illustrators solo 2026.

Daren Bader.notes: CORRECTED (sweep 7/2026): in-hand, was filed exception — actually clears via Spectrum Gold. Early prolific MTG artist.

Andrew Mar.notes: CORRECTED (sweep 7/2026): in-hand (Shivan Reef), was filed exception — actually clears via Spectrum Gold.

Allen Williams.credentials: Chesley wins: 2015 (Best Monochrome Unpublished, 'Sphynx'), 2017 (Best Monochrome, 'The Fall of Night').
Allen Williams.documentary_note: ImagineFX features (drawing/pencil tutorials); in the Enchanted book (plate).
Allen Williams.notes: ADDED (verification pass 7/2026): confirmed MTG (72 cards) + Chesley monochrome wins + Enchanted plate.

Sam Burley.credentials: Chesley win 2013 (Best Interior Illustration, 'Brother. Prince. Snake.').

Rick Berry.credentials: Spectrum GOLD Medal 1997 ('Wintermute') + 3 Spectrum Silver; Chesley wins 2000 ('Artemis') + 2005 ('Queen of the Amazons'); Society of Illustrators Silver 2010; WFA finalist x2.
Rick Berry.notes: ADDED (Koder batch). Pioneered digital painting (1st digital book cover, Neuromancer 1985). Koder-confirmed (Lucca). Work in Lucca Museum of Contemporary Art + Gaiman/King/GRRM collections.

rk post.credentials: OG MTG Lane adjacent — Exodus 1998 debut, long-tenured MTG artist. No traditional gate award; qualifies via OG-lane exception + Koder/Eidolon proxy.

rk post.notes: Merged duplicate ('Randy rk Post' + 'rk post' were the same artist). SCGCon LA Oct 9-11 2026 confirmed guest; Mirari's Wake (original Judgment printing) target. Prolific MTG + D&D + WoW.

Bill Sienkiewicz.credentials: No fantasy-art gate award; qualifies via comics canon (multiple Eisner Award wins — Elektra: Assassin, etc.). Eisner-winning monograph on his own work.
Bill Sienkiewicz.notes: ADDED (comics batch). Comics master (Elektra: Assassin, New Mutants, Moon Knight); ~18 MTG cards. lucas_narrative=proxy_eisner (6, = Chesley-Lifetime weight). Upgrade to 'confirmed' (18) if Lucas Museum acquires his work.
